title: Track Groups & Bus Routing
summary: Group related tracks into a single bus track. Select tracks > Cmd+G. The group track becomes the bus — add effects (Glue Compressor, EQ) to it to process all grouped tracks together.
steps: Select multiple tracks by clicking the first track header, then Shift+click the last
Press Cmd+G (Mac) to group them into a Group Track
Name the group by double-clicking its header (e.g. 'DRUMS', 'BASS', 'SYNTHS')
Add effects to the group track — they process the combined output of all grouped tracks
Common group FX: Glue Compressor on DRUMS group (bus glue). EQ Eight on BASS group (HP, shape the combined bass). Limiter on MASTER
Collapse the group by clicking the triangle on the group track to hide individual tracks and save screen space
tips: Standard grouping setup: DRUMS group (kick, snare, hats, perc) + Glue Compressor. BASS group (sub + mid bass) + EQ Eight. SYNTHS group (pads, stabs, leads) + EQ Eight + light Compressor
Colour-code groups: right-click a group header to assign a colour. Match the group colour to all tracks inside it — visual organisation in a complex session
The group track has its own fader — use it to balance the whole group in the mix. Lower the DRUMS group fader to bring the drums down without adjusting each drum level individually
Groups can be nested: you can have a BASS group inside a PRODUCTION group. Useful in complex sessions but unnecessary for garage builds
practice_hint: In rep-06 or any mini-track rep, select all drum tracks and press Cmd+G. Name the group 'DRUMS'. Add Glue Compressor to the group track. Set ratio 4:1, attack 3ms, release Auto. Compare the drum sound with Glue Compressor bypassed vs active.
ableton_version_note: Available in all editions of Live 9+.
